title: Smoking in Berlin: Quick Summary
items: **Main restaurant and café rooms are smoke-free.** Berlin allows narrow exceptions for a fully separated secondary smoking room or a marked one-room **Raucherkneipe** under 75 m² that meets every condition.

**Vaping is not described identically in every law.** Transport operators explicitly ban it outside designated areas; bars, restaurants and hotels can do the same through house rules. Ask or step outside.

**Public transport is smoke-free outside marked areas.** No smoking or vaping inside vehicles. Some open-air S-Bahn stations have designated smoking zones; enforcement and penalties depend on the operator.
**Outdoor tobacco smoking has no blanket citywide ban**, but terraces, schools, playgrounds, events and other sites can set stricter rules. Follow signs and house rules.

**Adults 18+ may possess up to 25 g of cannabis in public within the Act's limits.** Germany has no general recreational retail shops, and short-term visitors do not meet cultivation-association residence and membership rules.

**Public cannabis use is limited.** It is prohibited in the immediate presence of minors, in protected facilities and within sight of their entrances, and in or within sight of cultivation associations. Beyond 100 m from the relevant entrance it is no longer considered within sight; pedestrian zones are restricted from 07:00 to 20:00.
**There is no blanket safe park or side-street rule.** Check minors, entrances, signs, house rules and pedestrian-zone status; if unsure, do not consume in public.
